{{Quote|Switch to targeting computers and let's blow this thing!|Fin Danglot|<ref name="GG1-1" />}}
'''Fin Danglot''' was a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] who served in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="GG1-1" /> By [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> prior to fellow [[Rebel pilot/Legends|Rebel pilot]] [[Puck Naeco/Legends|Puck Naeco's]] [[Death/Legends|death]] during the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Naeco was a mentor for Danglot as well as pilots [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]] and [[Milar Travis]].<ref name="EGTW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], Danglot stood at a height of 1.65 [[Meter/Legends|meters]]. Danglot was equipped with a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] and a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], and wore the standard [[Rebel flight suit]] along with a [[Flight helmet/Legends|flight helmet]] and [[Goggles/Legends|goggles]] during their service as a Rebel pilot.<ref name="GG1-1" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|<nowiki />''[…]'' Red 12 finally gets a name, chosen after sorting through a number of candidates. They were Naeco (original ''X-wing'' game), Captain Ernek Marskan (same), Fin Danglot (''Galaxy Guide 1''), Travis (a blonde woman from Marvel's retelling of ''A New Hope'' back in the ''Droids'' kid's comic – how's ''that'' for obscure?), and Talos Merkin (''Captive to Evil'').|Jason Fry, ''The Essential Guide to Warfare'' endnotes|<ref name="Warfare">{{Tumblr|subdomain=jasonfry|url=post/24614452839/eg-to-warfare-endnotes-pt-10|name=Jason Fry's Dorkery|text=EG to Warfare: Endnotes Pt. 10|quote=[…] Red 12 finally gets a name, chosen after sorting through a number of candidates. They were Naeco (original ''X-wing'' game), Captain Ernek Marskan (same), Fin Danglot (''Galaxy Guide 1''), Travis (a blonde woman from Marvel's retelling of ''A New Hope'' back in the ''Droids'' kid's comic – how's ''that'' for obscure?), and Talos Merkin (''Captive to Evil'').}}</ref>}}
The character of Fin Danglot was first mentioned in [[1989]]'s first edition of [[West End Games]]' ''[[Galaxy Guide 1: A New Hope]]''.<ref name="GG1-1" /> Notably in the same year, the image accompanying the character entry was also used in West End Games' ''[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back]]'' to accompany the entry of another character, [[Tenk Lenso]].<ref name="GG3 1st">''[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back]]''</ref> [[Jason Fry]] considered Fin Danglot as one of several candidates to [[Retroactive continuity|retcon]] as the previously unnamed role of [[Red Twelve/Legends|Red Twelve]] during the Battle of Yavin for the [[2012]] reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''. He ultimately went with the Puck Naeco character. Nonetheless, both Danglot and Milar Travis were separately included in the book as a shoutout.<ref name="Warfare">{{Tumblr|subdomain=jasonfry|url=post/24614452839/eg-to-warfare-endnotes-pt-10|name=Jason Fry's Dorkery|text=EG to Warfare: Endnotes Pt. 10|quote=[…] Red 12 finally gets a name, chosen after sorting through a number of candidates. They were Naeco (original ''X-wing'' game), Captain Ernek Marskan (same), Fin Danglot (''Galaxy Guide 1''), Travis (a blonde woman from Marvel's retelling of ''A New Hope'' back in the ''Droids'' kid's comic – how's ''that'' for obscure?), and Talos Merkin (''Captive to Evil'').}}</ref>
